Quality & Certifications

Documentation is not an afterthought

The difference between a sample that converts and one that stalls in procurement is almost always documentation. We match every ingredient we source to the specific certification stack your market requires — and we deliver the paperwork your team needs to move fast.

Why certification depth matters for Indian botanicals

India has more GMP-certified botanical processing facilities than any other emerging market. Organic, Halal, Kosher, and ISO certifications are available at scale — across hundreds of producers. The challenge is not whether a certification exists. It's finding the producer who holds the right certification, at the right specification, for your specific market's import requirements.

Different markets require different certification stacks. An EU supplement brand needs something different from a Japanese functional food manufacturer. A UAE distributor needs something different from a US private-label producer. We know the difference — and we source accordingly.

GMP
ISO
FSSAI
Organic
Halal
Kosher
The Certifications

What each certification means — and who needs it

GMP

Good Manufacturing Practice

Required in: EU · UK · USA · Japan · Korea · ANZ · UAE/GCC

GMP certification confirms that a manufacturing facility operates to documented quality standards — covering premises, equipment, personnel, production processes, quality control, and record-keeping. For botanical ingredient buyers, it is the baseline certification that most regulated markets require before an ingredient can enter a finished product supply chain.

In India, GMP certification is issued by state licensing authorities under Schedule M of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act for Ayurvedic products, and by FSSAI for food-grade ingredients. Many Indian producers also hold WHO-GMP certification — which carries recognition across most international markets — and cGMP certification aligned with US FDA standards.

Ayris Global supplies: WHO-GMP, cGMP (FDA-aligned), and Schedule M certified producers across all six ingredient categories.
ISO

ISO 22000 / ISO 9001

Required in: EU · UK · USA · Japan · Korea · ANZ · all regulated markets

ISO 22000 is the international standard for food safety management systems — covering hazard analysis, prerequisite programmes, and documented quality controls across the full supply chain. For botanical ingredient buyers, it provides confidence that a producer's quality systems are independently audited and internationally recognised.

ISO 9001 covers the broader quality management system — process control, customer focus, continuous improvement. Together, ISO 22000 and ISO 9001 form the quality management backbone that most international procurement teams require from new ingredient suppliers.

Ayris Global supplies: ISO 22000 and ISO 9001 certified producers. Full ISO certificates provided as part of standard documentation packages.
FSSAI

Food Safety & Standards Authority of India

Required in: All Indian botanical exports · export compliance baseline

FSSAI licensing is the mandatory baseline for any Indian food or botanical ingredient producer intending to export. It confirms that the facility is registered with India's national food safety regulator and operates within India's Food Safety and Standards Act framework — covering hygiene, labelling, additives, and contaminant limits.

For international buyers, FSSAI licensing is a minimum filter — it tells you the producer is operating legally within India's regulatory framework. It does not replace market-specific certifications (GMP, ISO, Organic etc.) but is a foundational compliance indicator that all Ayris Global suppliers hold.

Ayris Global supplies: All producers hold current FSSAI licences. FSSAI documentation included in all shipment packages.
Organic

NPOP (India) / NOP (USA) / EU Organic

Required in: EU · UK · USA · ANZ · and wherever organic label claims are made

India's National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) is the country's government-run organic certification framework. NPOP-certified ingredients can be labelled as organic in export markets — and NPOP has mutual recognition agreements with the EU and Switzerland, meaning NPOP-certified produce can be marketed as organic in those markets without additional re-certification.

For the US market, NOP (National Organic Program) certification — or NPOP certification with NOP equivalency through a USDA-accredited certifier — is required to make organic claims on products sold in the United States. For ANZ, the Australian Certified Organic (ACO) standard is typically required, though some buyers accept NPOP with additional documentation.

Ayris Global supplies: NPOP and NOP certified organic ingredients across all six categories. EU organic equivalency confirmed where applicable. Organic certificates and transaction certificates provided per shipment.
Halal

Halal Certification

Required in: UAE/GCC · Malaysia · Indonesia · most SEA markets · UK · EU (Muslim consumer segments)

Halal certification confirms that an ingredient has been produced, processed, and handled in accordance with Islamic dietary law — covering raw material sourcing, processing aids, cross-contamination controls, and facility practices. For botanical ingredients, Halal certification is primarily relevant to processing and handling (animal-derived processing aids, alcohol use in extraction, facility hygiene protocols).

In the UAE and GCC markets, Halal certification is effectively mandatory for most food and supplement ingredients — required by the Emirates Standards and Metrology Authority (ESMA) and equivalent Gulf Standards Organisation bodies. In Malaysia and Indonesia, Halal certification from a JAKIM or MUI-recognised body respectively is required. The UK and EU are significant and growing Halal consumer markets where certification adds meaningful commercial value.

Ayris Global supplies: Halal-certified ingredients across all six categories, certified by recognised Indian Halal certification bodies with GCC and SEA market acceptance. Halal certificates provided per shipment.
Kosher

Kosher Certification

Required in: USA · EU · Israel · select global markets

Kosher certification confirms that an ingredient is produced in accordance with Jewish dietary law — covering ingredient sourcing, processing equipment, facility standards, and rabbinical supervision. For botanical ingredients, Kosher certification is largely relevant to processing controls and the absence of non-Kosher additives or processing aids.

The US is the world's largest Kosher market — and Kosher certification has long carried commercial value beyond the Jewish consumer segment, as it functions as an additional quality signal for a broader health-conscious consumer base. EU, Canadian, and Israeli markets also have meaningful Kosher demand. For premium supplement and wellness brands, Kosher certification can be a meaningful brand differentiator.

Ayris Global supplies: Kosher-certified ingredients available on request across select product lines. Please specify Kosher requirement in your sourcing enquiry.
What We Provide

The standard documentation package

Every shipment. No exceptions. No chasing.

CoA

Certificate of Analysis

Batch-specific analytical results — active content, microbiological limits, heavy metals, pesticide residues, moisture. Issued by accredited third-party laboratory.

MSDS

Material Safety Data Sheet

Physical and chemical properties, handling precautions, storage conditions, and emergency response information. Required by most regulated market importers.

Phyto

Phytosanitary Certificate

Issued by India's National Plant Protection Organisation (NPPO). Confirms the shipment is free from regulated pests and diseases. Required for botanical imports into most markets.

GMP

GMP Certificate

Current GMP certificate from the manufacturing facility — WHO-GMP, cGMP, or Schedule M as applicable to the ingredient and market.

Org

Organic Certificate + TC

For organic-certified ingredients: NPOP/NOP certificate plus Transaction Certificate (TC) per shipment — required to make organic claims in destination markets.

Halal/K

Halal / Kosher Certificate

Where applicable: current Halal or Kosher certificate from the certifying body, with scope of certification confirming the specific ingredient and facility.

Origin

Certificate of Origin

Issued by the relevant Indian export authority. Confirms Indian origin — and where applicable, supports preferential duty treatment under applicable trade frameworks.

Spec

Technical Data Sheet

Full product specification — botanical name, plant part, extraction method, standardisation parameters, physical characteristics, storage and shelf life. Available prior to sampling.

Certification by Market

Which certifications your market requires

A quick reference for procurement teams. Tell us your market and we'll ensure the right certification stack is in place.

Market GMP ISO FSSAI Organic Halal Kosher
European Union ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ NPOP equiv. ◦ ◦
United Kingdom ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ◦
United States ✓ cGMP ✓ ✓ ✓ NOP ◦ ◦
UAE / GCC ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ✓ Required ◦
Japan ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ◦ ◦
Korea ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ◦ ◦
ANZ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ◦
Southeast Asia ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ✓ Most markets ◦
Latin America ✓ ✓ ✓ ◦ ◦ ◦

✓ = typically required or strongly recommended. ◦ = market-specific or category-dependent. Always confirm with your regulatory team. Ayris Global can supply any combination on request.

Tell us your market. We'll handle the certification.

Specify your target market in your enquiry and we'll ensure every ingredient comes with the right certification stack — matched and confirmed before we send you a sample.
